title: Interpret Hot Bin Handling Behavior For Marked Totes
summary: Use the training source to interpret expected OptiSweep behavior when totes are marked as hot bin. The source describes hot bins as the highest priority bins and states that totes marked as hot bin will close chute and exchange.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing or verifying source-described OptiSweep behavior for totes marked as hot bin, including understanding priority and expected chute close and exchange behavior.
not_for: Do not use as a corrective action procedure.
Do not use to infer additional system logic, controls, or recovery actions beyond the training source description.
Do not use for hot bin disable behavior beyond what is explicitly stated in the supplied source evidence.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Review the training-described hot bin indicator context and verify whether observed tote handling matches the documented hot bin behavior.

safety_notes: This source is training-oriented and describes behavior rather than a full operator workflow.
Do not infer additional corrective actions or system logic beyond the source description.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the training source or documented hot bin behavior
Ability to observe or verify tote handling behavior
Indicator or message reference if available in the operating environment

access_required: Access to the training source or documented hot bin behavior
system_preconditions: A tote or handling scenario marked as hot bin is being reviewed or observed.
Indicator or message reference is available if present in the operating environment.

visual_references.description: Training frame describing hot bins as highest priority bins and stating that totes marked as hot bin close chute and exchange.

steps.title: Identify hot bin marking context
steps.purpose: Establish that the tote or handling scenario being reviewed is the hot bin case described by the source.
steps.instruction: Identify that the tote or bin handling being discussed is marked as hot bin using the source-described indicator or message reference.
steps.expected_result: The reviewed tote or scenario is recognized as the hot bin case referenced by the training source.
steps.healthy_condition: A hot bin marking or indicator-related reference is present and aligns with the training description.
steps.failure_condition: No hot bin marking, indicator reference, or source-supported basis is available to confirm the scenario is a hot bin case.

actions.label: Identify hot bin case
actions.exact_action: Identify that the tote or bin handling being discussed is marked as hot bin using the source-described indicator or message reference.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the scenario cannot be tied to a hot bin marking or indicator reference from the source.
Escalate if the available system context does not provide a source-supported way to identify the hot bin case.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: They send us a message saying what's hopped in; the message type is called the indicator.
Artifact covers hot bins and related indicator context.

steps.title: Confirm hot bin priority
steps.purpose: Verify the source-defined operational meaning of a hot bin.
steps.instruction: Confirm from the source that hot bins are treated as the highest priority bins for urgent handling.
steps.expected_result: The user understands that hot bins are the highest priority bins in the described context.
steps.healthy_condition: The source review confirms hot bins are described as highest priority bins.
steps.failure_condition: The reviewed material does not support or clearly show the highest-priority interpretation.

actions.label: Review hot bin priority statement
actions.exact_action: Confirm from the source that hot bins are treated as the highest priority bins for urgent handling.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Text or transcript context stating that hot bins are the highest priority bins.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the observed or taught behavior conflicts with the source statement that hot bins are highest priority.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Hot bins will trigger all totes that are marked as hot bin to close chute and exchange; hot bins are described as the highest priority bins.

success_criteria: The user can interpret that totes marked as hot bin are handled as highest-priority items.
The user can identify that the source describes hot bin totes as triggering chute close and exchange behavior.
The indicator message reference is captured using source-supported wording.
healthy_conditions: Hot bins are understood from the source as the highest priority bins.
Totes marked as hot bin are expected to close chute and exchange according to the source.
Observed or reviewed behavior matches the source description when verification is performed.
failure_conditions: Observed system behavior does not match the documented hot bin behavior in the source.
The scenario cannot be confirmed as a hot bin case using source-supported indicator or message context.
Additional corrective logic or actions would be required but are not supported by the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if observed system behavior does not match the documented hot bin behavior in the source.
Do not infer additional corrective actions or system logic beyond the source description.
